scope.objective: Escalate from trivia (uid 1000) to root via sudo facter privilege escalation. Read /root/root.txt.
in_scope: 10.129.6.121 via SSH as trivia (key: /tmp/trivia_key, no passphrase)
sudo /usr/bin/facter — runs as ALL with NOPASSWD
Facter custom fact loading for Ruby code execution
Local privilege escalation techniques on the target host
stop_conditions: root shell obtained and root.txt read
objective exhausted — facter approach fails after 3 attempts

primary_path: Facter custom facts: Create a malicious Ruby fact file in a custom facts directory (e.g., /tmp/facts/), then run 'sudo /usr/bin/facter --custom-dir /tmp/facts/' to execute arbitrary Ruby code as root. The fact file should contain a Facter.add block with system() or exec() calls. Read /root/root.txt.
backup_path: If --custom-dir flag is restricted, try FACTERLIB environment variable: 'sudo FACTERLIB=/tmp/facts /usr/bin/facter'. Also try: facter loads facts from ~/.puppetlabs/opt/puppet/cache/lib/facter/ or /opt/puppetlabs/puppet/cache/lib/facter/. Check 'facter --help' for all options.

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: Arbitrary Ruby code execution via sudo facter — facter is a Ruby-based tool that loads and evaluates Ruby files from custom fact directories
delivery_forms: Custom fact via FACTERLIB env var: sudo FACTERLIB=/tmp/facts /usr/bin/facter
Custom fact placed in default fact search paths
Direct Ruby execution if facter is a Ruby script: manipulate RUBYLIB or GEM_PATH
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: None — sudo grants NOPASSWD access with no env restrictions visible. env_reset is set but FACTERLIB may still pass through.
untested_forms: FACTERLIB environment variable passthrough with sudo
Default custom fact directories writeable by trivia

notes: SSH access: ssh -i /tmp/trivia_key trivia@10.129.6.121 (key has no passphrase, already decrypted). The trivia user has sudo NOPASSWD on /usr/bin/facter. Facter is a Puppet tool that evaluates Ruby facts files. Write a malicious .rb fact file and point facter to it. This should give immediate root code execution.
